steps.title: Identify what needs to be explained
steps.content:
Sehasseh et al. attribute the beads' apparent ______ to two things: (1) the evolution and spread of symbolic behavior in humans, and (2) the widespread availability of T. gibbosula shells during the MSA. The blank must name what those two causes explain about the beads — something consistent with finding them in "a plethora" of sites "across western North Africa."

The blank describes a geographic/distributional quality of the beads. Test each choice:
  • Choice A — 'heterogeneity': means variety or diversity. The beads are consistently fashioned from the same shell; the text emphasizes their abundance and spread, not variation among them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'ubiquity': means widespread presence. Finding a plethora of these beads across many sites across western North Africa is precisely what "ubiquity" describes, and both causes explain why they are so widespread. ✓
  • Choice C — 'expediency': means practicality or convenience. Nothing in the text frames the beads as a convenient or practical solution to a problem. ✗
  • Choice D — 'fecundity': means fertility or reproductive abundance. The text does not connect the beads to any fertility theme. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
Ubiquity is the only word that captures the pattern described — beads found at site after site across a wide region — and is logically explained by both the spread of symbolic behavior and the shells' natural availability.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
